tasks.request: Go through every provider and pharmacy record and give one clean medication list
tasks.summary: Nine medications verified across three providers and Fred Meyer Pharmacy. One dose conflict (metoprolol at Dr. Sharma's), one manufacturer label difference (atorvastatin generic), one refill-count flag (apixaban). Clean list ready to print and carry to each appointment.

tasks.request: When two records disagree, go with the newest and most authoritative, and tell me which one you trusted and which you set aside
tasks.summary: Seven conflicts documented across medical, financial, and calendar domains. Each resolution names the trusted source, the set-aside source, and the reasoning.

tasks.request: Make sure the HbA1c draw is booked the week before endocrinology
tasks.summary: HbA1c must be drawn between September 28 and October 2 for the October 6 endocrinology appointment. Booking not yet confirmed - Carlos needs to call Westside Diabetes and Endocrine Clinic or Quest Diagnostics to schedule.

tasks.request: Look at whether anything else has to happen before the cardiology visit in November
tasks.summary: Three pre-visit tasks identified: (1) Confirm Karen's ride by Nov 9, (2) Bring medication list and BP log, (3) Request new apixaban prescription at the visit. No lab work required beforehand.

tasks.request: Reconcile what I spent this year against what I planned to spend, category by category
tasks.summary: Spending is $681 under budget YTD through September. Biggest variance: prescription line was overestimated by $92/month. Groceries slightly over (+$163 YTD). Net monthly surplus averaging $1,494 vs budgeted $1,418.

tasks.request: Look at prescriptions especially hard - budget line was set before the February change
tasks.summary: Actual prescription spending is ~$36/month vs budgeted $185/month. The atorvastatin generic switch saved $26/year. The metoprolol dose increase added $6/year. Net prescriptions are $20 under the pre-change baseline and far under the overestimated budget.

tasks.request: Find out what replacing a furnace actually costs and whether savings can absorb it
tasks.summary: Furnace replacement for a 1,400 sq ft ranch in Beaverton: $4,500–$8,500 depending on efficiency tier. Savings ($38,143) handles all scenarios without borrowing or IRA withdrawal. Recovery time: 3–6 months at current surplus. No contractors contacted - Carlos decides when.

tasks.request: Reconcile Garden Club books - every donation and expense against card reader and bank
tasks.summary: Club took in $2,270 and spent $1,261.80 through September. Bank balance $2,850.70 matches Carlos's ledger to the penny. Square card reader deposits reconciled across three events. Every number is defensible. Report ready for Maggie and the board.

tasks.request: Calendar from October through January in one place - every appointment, family date, and standing commitment
tasks.summary: Full four-month view with 3 medical appointments, 4 birthdays, 1 memorial date, 1 art show, 1 shipping deadline, ~15 workshops, ~17 Kevin calls, ~8 Karen dinners, 4 Doug lunches, 4 Garden Club meetings. Nothing hiding behind something else.

open_action_items: Book HbA1c blood draw for week of September 28 - October 2
Confirm Karen's ride for November 11 cardiology (by November 9)
Call Riverview Medical Partners to update metoprolol to 50 mg (before December 9)
Request new apixaban Rx at November 11 cardiology visit
Check with Karen about Ethan's fall soccer schedule
Schedule annual audiology check at Northwest Hearing Center (December)
Decide on furnace replacement timing - no contractor contacted yet
